item.dc:creator: Ben Kamisar
item.description: Major general election matchups across the House battleground are set after Tuesday’s primaries, as the race for control of Congress takes center stage in the midterm elections.

media:content.media:text: Scranton Mayor Paige Cognetti speaks during a President Joe Biden campaign event in Scranton, Pa., Tuesday, April 16, 2024.
media:content.media:description: Scranton, Pa., Mayor Paige Cognetti in Scranton in 2024.

item.title: Democrats pick candidates for key districts in the battle for the House

item.description: Navy veteran Ed Gallrein praised President Trump during his victory speech after defeating Rep. Thomas Massie in Kentucky's 4th Congressional District. Gallrein added that the winners are “the Republicans of Kentucky congressional district four and their families,” which he says he “will be a champion for in Washington."

media:content.media:title: Gallrein praises 'courageous' Trump in Kentucky primary victory speech
media:content.media:description: Navy veteran Ed Gallrein praised President Trump during his victory speech after defeating Rep. Thomas Massie in Kentucky's 4th Congressional District. Gallrein added that the winners are “the Republicans of Kentucky congressional district four and their families,” which he says he “will be a champion for in Washington."

item.title: Gallrein praises 'courageous' Trump in Kentucky primary victory speech

item.dc:creator: Henry J. Gomez
item.description: Former Navy SEAL Ed Gallrein has won the Republican primary in Kentucky’s 4th Congressional District over Rep. Thomas Massie.

media:content.media:text: Rep. Thomas Massie and challenger Ed Gallrein
media:content.media:description: Rep. Thomas Massie and challenger Ed Gallrein

item.title: Trump-endorsed Ed Gallrein unseats Rep. Thomas Massie in Kentucky GOP primary

item.dc:creator: Alexandra Marquez
item.description: Two Georgia Supreme Court justices won re-election on Tuesday, NBC News projects, fending off Democratic-backed challengers in the battleground state.

media:content.media:text: A split composite image of Charles Bethel, left, and Sarah Warren, both wearing black judges robes
media:content.media:description: Georgia Supreme Court Justices Charles Bethel and Sarah Warren each won another six-year term on Tuesday.

item.title: Georgia Supreme Court justices fend off Democratic-backed challengers

item.dc:creator: Bridget Bowman
item.description: The Republican nominee will take on Democratic Sen. Jon Ossoff in November in a critical battleground race.

media:content.media:description: Rep. Mike Collins in Peachtree City, Ga., in August.

media:content.media:text: Former football coach and Republican candidate for US Senate Derek Dooley
media:content.media:description: Rep. Mike Collins in Peachtree City, Ga., in August.

item.title: Rep. Mike Collins and Derek Dooley advance to Georgia GOP Senate runoff

item.dc:creator: Adam Edelman
item.description: NBC News projects the former Atlanta mayor and Biden administration official cleared the 50% threshold needed to avoid a runoff.

media:content.media:description: Keisha Lance Bottoms, former mayor of Atlanta, at Hotel Phoenix in Atlanta, Georgia, US, on Wednesday, March 18, 2026.

item.title: Keisha Lance Bottoms wins Democratic nomination in Georgia governor’s race

item.dc:creator: Adam Edelman
item.description: Georgia Lt. Gov. Burt Jones and billionaire Rick Jackson will advance to a June runoff in the state’s Republican primary for governor, NBC News projects

media:content.media:description: Republican candidates for governor, Rick Jackson, left, and Lt. Gov. Burt Jones interact at the Atlanta Press Club Loudermilk-Young Republican governor primary election debate at Georgia Public Broadcasting, Monday, April 27, 2026, in Atlanta.

item.title: Republicans Burt Jones and Rick Jackson advance to a runoff in the Georgia governor primary, setting up a MAGA clash

item.dc:creator: Ryan J. Reilly
item.description: Months before the $1.7 billion fund was announced, Justice Department official Ed Martin said Capitol rioters would get millions, even if it took until 2028.

media:content.media:credit: Craig Hudson for The Washington Post via Getty Images file
media:content.media:text: Ed Martin speaks during a press conference
media:content.media:description: Supporters of President Donald Trump rally at the U.S. Capitol on Jan. 6, 2021.
Supporters of President Donald Trump rally at the U.S. Capitol on Jan. 6, 2021.
Supporters of President Donald Trump rally at the U.S. Capitol on Jan. 6, 2021.

item.title: DOJ official told GOP ally that big payouts were coming for Jan. 6 defendants

item.description: Authorities said they are analyzing a potential manifesto from the two suspected gunmen. Plus, we’re learning more about the heroic actions of the victims. NBC News’ Morgan Chesky reports.

media:content.media:title: New details in deadly mosque attack
media:content.media:description: Authorities said they are analyzing a potential manifesto from the two suspected gunmen. Plus, we’re learning more about the heroic actions of the victims. NBC News’ Morgan Chesky reports.

item.title: New details in deadly mosque attack

item.dc:creator: Maggie Vespa
Denise Chow
item.description: By the time the American surgeon who contracted Ebola was flown to Germany, he was barely able to stand on his own, according to leaders of the Christian missionary group where he worked.

media:content.media:text: The Stafford Family. Photograph has been blurred by Serge News.
media:content.media:description: Drs. Rebekah and Peter Stafford with their children. The photograph has been blurred by Serge.

item.title: American doctor with Ebola evacuated to Germany as wife and kids are monitored in Congo

item.dc:creator: Frank Thorp V
Brennan Leach
Sahil Kapur
item.description: The Senate voted to move forward with a resolution to force President Donald Trump to end the war in Iran, a breakthrough for the Democratic-led effort.

media:content.media:description: "Until the administration provides clarity, no congressional authorization or extension can be justified," said Sen. Bill Cassidy, R-La..

item.title: Senate advances resolution to end Iran war as GOP Sen. Bill Cassidy flips to support it

item.dc:creator: Michael Kosnar
Kelly O'Donnell
Tim Stelloh
Nicole Acevedo
item.description: A U.S. indictment of former Cuban president Raúl Castro is expected to be announced Wednesday in Miami, two federal sources familiar with the investigation told NBC News

media:content.media:description: Raul Castro in Havana on May 1, 2026.

item.title: Raúl Castro is expected to be indicted by U.S. on Wednesday, sources say

item.description: NBC News projects that Virginia voters have approved redrawing the congressional district map. NBC News' Steve Kornacki provides analysis of the vote.

media:content.media:title: Virginia voters approve congressional redistricting amendment
media:content.media:description: NBC News projects that Virginia voters have approved redrawing the congressional district map. NBC News' Steve Kornacki provides analysis of the vote.

item.title: Virginia voters approve congressional redistricting amendment

item.description: On the sidelines of the Trump-Xi Summit in Beijing, NBC News' Tom Llamas sits down with Secretary of State Marco Rubio to discuss China policy, the war with Iran, the future of Cuba, and more.

media:content.media:title: Extended Interview: Tom Llamas sits down with Secretary of State Marco Rubio
media:content.media:description: On the sidelines of the Trump-Xi Summit in Beijing, NBC News' Tom Llamas sits down with Secretary of State Marco Rubio to discuss China policy, the war with Iran, the future of Cuba, and more.

item.title: Extended Interview: Tom Llamas sits down with Secretary of State Marco Rubio

item.dc:creator: Megan Lebowitz
item.description: NBC News projects Drazan, a state senator, emerged from a 14-candidate field to advance to the general election in the blue state.

media:content.media:description: Oregon Republican gubernatorial candidate Christine Drazan.

item.title: Christine Drazan wins Oregon GOP primary for governor, setting up rematch with Democratic Gov. Tina Kotek

item.dc:creator: Matt Dixon
Bridget Bowman
Henry J. Gomez
item.description: President Donald Trump flexed the strength of his party-transforming political movement again, continuing the successful process of eliminating political enemies within the GOP this month.

media:content.media:description: President Donald Trump arrives to speak in Hebron, Ky., in March.

item.title: Trump notches more GOP primary wins, but big midterm questions remain

item.dc:creator: Alicia Victoria Lozano
item.description: The charge stems from an incident on April 2, when Keith Ervin hugged the girl, a student member of the board, and told her, “God, you’re hot.”

media:content.media:text: Keith Ervin gives a statement during the Washington County Schools Board of Education Emergency Meeting on April 8, 2026.
media:content.media:description: Keith Ervin gives a statement at a Washington County Schools Board of Education emergency meeting on April 8.

item.title: School board member who hugged teen and called her ‘hot’ is charged with assault

item.dc:creator: Megan Lebowitz
item.description: NBC News projects Moore didn’t clear the 50% threshold to advance to the general election. His runoff opponent remains unclear.

media:content.media:description: Rep. Barry Moore, R-Ala.
Rep. Barry Moore, R-Ala.
Rep. Barry Moore, R-Ala.

item.title: Trump-backed Rep. Barry Moore heads to a runoff in Alabama GOP Senate primary

item.dc:creator: Garrett Haake
Raquel Coronell Uribe
item.description: The former Department of Health and Human Services spokesperson filed the claim a day after the Justice Department set up the nearly $1.8 billion fund.

media:content.media:text: Former Trump campaign official Michael Caputo
media:content.media:description: Michael Caputo says his family "lost everything" because of a federal probe.

item.title: Longtime Trump ally Michael Caputo files first known claim for ‘anti-weaponization’ fund

item.dc:creator: Ryan J. Reilly
item.description: Opponents of a $1.776 billion taxpayer-backed “anti-weaponization” fund projected a quote from one of the Founding Fathers onto the Justice Department building in protest.

media:content.media:text: A quotation from John Adams was projected on the side of "Main Justice" on Tuesday night.
media:content.media:description: A quotation from John Adams was projected on the side of "Main Justice" on Tuesday night.
A quotation from John Adams was projected on the side of "Main Justice" on Tuesday night.

item.title: John Adams quote projected on DOJ building in protest of $1.8B fund

item.dc:creator: Adam Edelman
item.description: NBC News projects the former Atlanta mayor and Biden administration official cleared the 50% threshold needed to avoid a runoff.

media:content.media:description: Keisha Lance Bottoms, former mayor of Atlanta, at Hotel Phoenix in Atlanta, Georgia, US, on Wednesday, March 18, 2026.

item.title: Keisha Lance Bottoms wins Democratic nomination in Georgia governor’s race

item.dc:creator: Ben Kamisar
item.description: House Democrats are condemning Texas candidate Maureen Galindo for saying she’d turn an immigration detention center into a “prison for American Zionists” if she won.

media:content.media:description: Maureen Galindo, a Democratic House candidate in Texas, faces backlash from both parties over antisemitic comments.

item.title: House Democratic leaders condemn Texas candidate for antisemitic comments

item.dc:creator: Bridget Bowman
item.description: The Republican nominee will take on Democratic Sen. Jon Ossoff in November in a critical battleground race.

media:content.media:description: Rep. Mike Collins in Peachtree City, Ga., in August.

media:content.media:text: Former football coach and Republican candidate for US Senate Derek Dooley
media:content.media:description: Rep. Mike Collins in Peachtree City, Ga., in August.

item.title: Rep. Mike Collins and Derek Dooley advance to Georgia GOP Senate runoff

item.dc:creator: Sahil Kapur
item.description: Sen. Tommy Tuberville won the Republican primary for governor of Alabama on Tuesday, NBC News projects, making him the clear favorite to win the general election in the ruby-red state this fall

media:content.media:description: Sen. Tommy Tuberville, R-Ala., at the Capitol on March 3.

item.title: Sen. Tommy Tuberville wins Republican primary for governor of Alabama

item.dc:creator: Alexandra Marquez
item.description: Two Georgia Supreme Court justices won re-election on Tuesday, NBC News projects, fending off Democratic-backed challengers in the battleground state.

media:content.media:text: A split composite image of Charles Bethel, left, and Sarah Warren, both wearing black judges robes
media:content.media:description: Georgia Supreme Court Justices Charles Bethel and Sarah Warren each won another six-year term on Tuesday.

item.title: Georgia Supreme Court justices fend off Democratic-backed challengers
